title: finding.v1 — the finding base every check and review uses
description: The shared shape every finding uses, whatever produces it — a check, a review, or the engine's self-monitoring. A finding has three parts: a severity, a message, and a location. Other parts of the engine build on this shape, reusing it and adding their own details (for example, self-monitoring records where a signal came from and when it was first and last seen). This file is reference data only: nothing checks a finding against it yet — that enforcement arrives with the engine's validation step — and the file's own correctness as a schema is checked automatically.

severity.description: How serious the finding is. What the values mean depends on what produced the finding, so this base does not fix a list — each producer defines its own. It is an internal field, never shown to a person as-is; anything an operator sees is translated into plain language first. The base only requires that a severity is present, as text.

message.description: A clear, complete, plain-language statement of the finding — what is wrong, why it matters, and how to fix it — written so both a person and the engine can act on it, explaining any technical term rather than assuming it. The schema requires a message to be present; whether it truly meets that clarity bar is a judgment, not something this check can measure.

not_applicable.description: Optional. True when this finding is a disclosed no-op — a check reporting that it does not apply in this context and there is nothing to do (never a silent skip). Absent or false means the finding is actionable. A reader that groups findings uses this to keep dormant 'nothing to do' notes from burying the actionable ones; nothing depends on it being present, so a finding without it is treated as actionable.

location.description: Where the finding is, or null when it has no file location (for example, a finding about a whole document or one not tied to a file). When present, it points to a file and, optionally, a line.

file.description: Path of the file the finding concerns, relative to the repository root.

line.description: Line number within the file (counting from 1), or null when the finding is not tied to a specific line.
